rulings.Case Title: SIDNEY CASTRO VS. RAHUL KUMAR
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 16, 2019, Line 4, DEFENDANT RAHUL KUMAR MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. [Part 1 of 2] Defendant Kumar's motion for summary adjudication is denied. The Court has previously ruled that the cause of action is well-pled. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether Defendant was consciously aware that his actions created a substantial risk of tremendous harm to others. (See Civ. Code § 3294.) The evidence discloses that: Defendant had been driving for 17 hours prior to the accident (Richholt Decl., 2); Defendant was involved in an accident approximately two hours before hitting Plaintiff because Defendant "[t]ook [his] eyes off the road" while driving on the freeway at a high rate of speed (Kumar Depo., 44:5; Ex. E-1); after the first accident, Defendant was told to return to the taxicab lot (Nguyen Depo., 36:22-37:2); and Defendant did not return to the lot and continued to drive despite being impaired. It also shows that Defendant drove directly into Plaintiff's legs while Plaintiff was clearly visible and opening the rear door of his parked cab. (Exs. E-2, E-3.) Finally, Defendant himself acknowledged that "it's not good to drive impaired," including while fatigued, and that fatigue impairs a driver's ability to be aware of his surroundings and increases the risk of an accident in which someone could get hurt. (Kumar Depo, 20-23, 25.) A reasonable trier of fact could conclude that Defendant knew that he was impaired, posed a serious risk of injury, and consciously chose to act dangerously. (See Pacific Gas and Electric Company v. Superior Court (2018) 24 Cal.App.5th 1150, 1159 ["Conscious disregard means that the defendant was aware of the probable dangerous consequences of his conduct, and that he willfully and deliberately failed to avoid those consequences. Put another way, the defendant must have actual knowledge of the risk of harm it is creating and, in the face of that knowledge, fail to take steps it knows will reduce or eliminate the risk of harm." (citations and internal quotations omitted).]; see also Taylor v. Superior Court (1979) 24 Cal.3d 890, 895-896 ["the plaintiff must establish that the defendant was aware of the probable dangerous consequences of his conduct, and that he willfully and deliberately failed to avoid those consequences."].) "It is axiomatic that driving while very tired can lead to the driver falling asleep and having an accident." (Delamarter v. Couglar (M.D. Pa. Feb. 20, 2018) 2018 WL 950201, at *4-*5 [denying defendant truck driver's motion for summary judgment on claim for punitive damages, where there was evidence that driver knew he was sleepy and yet continued driving nonetheless, thereby making "a conscious decision to ignore the grave danger posed to other motorists and their passengers."]; see also Morrison v. Townley (1969) 269 Cal.App.2d 863, 869 ["a motorist who should realize that he is falling asleep, or may very likely be unable to stay awake, and yet continues at the wheel, may be found to be grossly negligent, guilty of reckless operation, or of willful or wanton misconduct'"].) In Purnick v. C.R. England Inc. 269 F.3d 851 (7th Cir.2001), the court held that the plaintiff could not recover punitive damages against a fatigued driver. However, the facts of that case are distinguishable. There, the evidence disclosed that the defendant driver "did not drive for the seventeen hours before the trip that ended in the crash-evidence that tends to show that [the defendant driver] likely thought he was rested." (Id. at 853.) [End of part 1 of 2] =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: R. MARTIN ROSCHEISEN ET AL VS. DEVLIN/MCNALLEY CONSTRUCTION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 16, 2019, Line 9, DEFENDANT RESCUE ROOFING CO's Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement Defendant and Cross-Complainant Rescue Roofing Co.'s motion for determination of good faith settlement is denied without prejudice to its renewal upon a proper evidentiary showing. Although Rescue Roofing asserts this is a "no-liability" case and that "none of Plaintiffs' alleged damages implicate Rescue's narrow scope of work," it has not presented any admissible evidence that would enable the Court to determine "a rough approximation of plaintiffs' total recovery and the settlor's proportionate liability." (Tech-Bilt, Inc. v., Woodward-Clyde & Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499.) "If 'there is no substantial evidence to support a critical assumption as to the nature and extent of a settling defendant's liability, then a determination of good faith based upon such assumption is an abuse of discretion.' " (Mattco Forge, Inc. v. Arthur Young & Co. (1995) 38 Cal.App.4th 1337, 1348 [settling parties did not proffer any evidence regarding their proportionate liability for plaintiff's alleged injury, but merely presented a series of "questionable assumptions" in their memorandum to show the settlement amount was reasonable].) Even if counsel had personal knowledge of such information, counsel's declaration does not provide any such evidence. (See Greshko v. County of Los Angeles (1987) 194 Cal.App.3d 822, 834 [attorney's declaration regarding settling defendant's liability insufficient where he failed to provide specific supporting facts or expert opinion].) While Rescue Roofing is correct that the party asserting the lack of good faith has the burden on that issue (Code Civ. Proc. § 877.6(d)), the moving party must provide the court in the first instance with a sufficient evidentiary basis upon which to make a good faith determination. (See Code Civ. Proc. § 877.6(b) ["The issue of the good faith of a settlement may be determined on the basis of affidavits served with the notice of hearing, and any counteraffidavits filed in response . . . ." (emphasis added)].) Rescue Roofing has not done so. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of Consent Judgment As To Defendant Pacific Coast Manufacturing, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 16, 2019, Line 10, PLAINTIFF ECOLOGICAL RIGHTS FOUNDATION's Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of Consent Judgment As To Defendant Pacific Coast Manufacturing, Inc. Plaintiff Ecological Rights Foundation's motion to approve Proposition 65 settlement is granted. No opposition filed and the settlement complies with the requirements for Proposition 65 settlements under Health & Safety Code section 25249.7. The Court finds that the warning that is required by the settlement complies with Health & Safety Code sections 25249.5-25249.14; the award of attorney's fees is reasonable under California law; and the penalty amount is reasonable based on the criteria set forth in Health & Safety Code section 25249.7(b)(2). rulings.Calendar Matter: Hearing Re: Defendant Howard Wong'S Application To Extend And Or Stay Discovery. (Motion Set Per Ex Parte Order Filed On 7-1-19)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 16, 2019, Line 12, Hearing Re: Defendant Howard Wong'S Application To Extend And Or Stay Discovery. (Motion Set Per Ex Parte Order Filed On 7-1-19) The Court agrees with the recommendation of the judge pro tem that appointment of a discovery referee is warranted by the exceptional circumstances presented by this case. The parties shall be prepared to address the process and schedule for identification and appointment of an appropriate referee, including whether either party claims economic inability to pay a pro rata share of the referee's fee. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: GRACE IMAGE PHOTOGRAPHY ET AL VS. JENI FONG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash Subpoenas, Request For Sanctions And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Same
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 16, 2019, Line 12, PLAINTIFF GRACE IMAGE PHOTOGRAPHY, JUDY MCKIE Notice Of Motion To Quash Subpoenas, Request For Sanctions The Court adopts the Report and Recommendation of the judge pro tem in its entirety. Plaintiffs' motion to quash subpoenas is granted in part and denied in part, as indicated in the summary of recommendations, subject to the conditions and limitations contained in the report. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: CERTAIN UNDERWRITERS AT LLOYD'S LONDON ET AL VS. CONNECT ART INTRNATIONAL, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion To Compel Depositions Of Rob Evans And Phil Harding And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: July 16, 2019 Line 6, DEFENDANT CONNECT ART INTRNATIONAL, LLC'S Motion To Compel Depositions Of Rob Evans And Phil Harding And Request For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Unless otherwise directed by the Court at the ex-parte hearing set for July 15, 2019 in Department 302, the Motion to Compel Deposition is continued to August 6, 2019 at 9:00 am in Dept. 301 commencing at 9:00 am. Both parties failed to comply with San Francisco Superior Court Local Rule 2.7(B). Assuming the motion proceeds on August 6, 2019 courtesy copies of all motion pleadings shall be delivered to Department 301 no later than July 26, 2019. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (JPT/302).

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL S. HOSHIYAMA VS. JOHN GARCIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Further Response To Written Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanction, Supporting Declaration Of Charles J. Katz, And Memorandum
rulings.Rulings: July 16, 2019 Line 7, DEFENDANT JOHN GARCIA'S Motion For Order Compelling Further Response To Written Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion to compel a further response to Form Interrogatories 50.1 and 50.2 is unopposed and granted. Following service of this Order with Notice of Entry the parties shall meet and confer by phone or in person referable to the information missing from plaintiff's initial interrogatory response and the form of delivery of same. If necessary a further verified response shall be served within thirty days after completion of the meet and confer process. Defendant's request for monetary sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (JPT/302).

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT MARSILI VS. CECILIA STONE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: July 16, 2019 Line 9, DEFENDANTS RICKEY LIU, RICKEY L. LIU, CPA, A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And Request For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants' Motion to Compel Discovery Responses and for Sanctions is granted. Within twenty days of service of a copy of this Order with Notice of Entry. Plaintiff, Robert Marsili shall serve full and complete verified responses without objection to Special Interrogatories, Set One, and Request for Production of Documents, Set One. The record reflects the discovery pleadings were served on April 9, 2019. Responses were due on May 14, 2019. A follow up communication referencing the overdue responses sent on May 21, 2019 was unanswered. This motion was filed and served on May 29, 2019 setting the hearing for July 2, 2019. The motion was unopposed and the Tentative Ruling was to grant the motion. On July 1, 2019 the attorney for plaintiff contacted the Clerk of Dept. 301 representing he had not received a copy of the motion papers. The Tentative Ruling was withdrawn and the motion continued for hearing on July 16. The Court contacted the respective attorneys by email advising of the continuance and directed counsel to the meet and confer on or before July 9, 2019. The issues raised by the motion were not resolved. The plaintiff does not oppose the motion. A monetary sanction in the amount of $1,260.00 is awarded to the defendants pursuant to C.C.P. 2023.010(d) to be paid within the aforesaid twenty days. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (JPT/302).

rulings.Case Title: MARTA GREENBERG ET AL VS. DIGITAL MEDIA SOLUTIONS LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 16, 2019, Line 13, DEFENDANT DIGITAL MEDIA SOLUTIONS LLC DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Defendant Digital Media Solutions LLC's demurrer to the complaint by plaintiffs Marta Greenberg, John Judge, Karen Mandel, Andrew Monroe, and Katie Van Cleave is overruled. Plaintiffs' complaint states a cause of action for violation of the restrictions on unsolicited commercial e-mail advertisers set forth in Business & Professions Code § 17529.5(a)(2). The Court's full tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: SAMORN SELIM VS. HASH MAP LABS, INC DBA APP ACADEMY, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 16, 2019, Line 11, DEFENDANTS HASH MAP LABS, INC., KUSH PATEL, KRITI PARASHAR's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Defendants Hash Map Labs, Inc. dba App Academy, Kush Patel, and Kriti Parashar's demurrers to Plaintiff Samorn Selim's complaint are sustained without leave to amend as to the eighth and ninth causes of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress (IIED) and negligent infliction of emotional distress (NIED) and the tenth cause of action for defamation. The court's full tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)
